steps.title: Write the ticket-count equation
steps.content: $x$ is the number of 80-dollar tickets and $y$ is the number of 240-dollar tickets. Together they total 130 tickets: $$x + y = 130.$$

steps.title: Write the revenue equation
steps.content: The 80-dollar tickets bring in $80x$ dollars and the 240-dollar tickets bring in $240y$ dollars, for a total of 28,800 dollars: $$80x + 240y = 28,800.$$

steps.title: Eliminate the answer choices
steps.content:
• A pairs $x + y = 130$ with $80x + 240y = 28,800$ — matches our system.
  • B uses $130 \times 28,800$ on the right, inflating the revenue total by a factor of 130.
  • C sets $x + y = 28,800$, which would mean 28,800 tickets sold, not 28,800 dollars.
  • D sets $80x = 240y$ (equal revenues) and $130x + 130y = 28,800$, neither of which captures 130 tickets sold for 28,800 dollars total.
That confirms A is correct.
